21.reason: Error: This resets count to 1 for every new character, but should only set count to 1 when starting a new word. For subsequent characters of the same word, count should not be reset. This causes incorrect counting when words are later merged.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: This resets count to 1 for every new character, even if the word already exists. It should only set count to 1 when a new word is first created, but the logic for handling duplicate words is flawed.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: This resets count to 1 for every new character, but should only set count to 1 when starting a new word. For subsequent characters of the same word, count should not be reset. This causes incorrect counting when words are later merged.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  This line incorrectly resets the count to 1 for every character added to the current word, instead of initializing it only once when a new word starts. It should be guarded by a check if k2 == 0.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: The count is reset to 1 for every character added to the current word. It should only be initialized when starting a new word (k2==0). This logic is flawed but might not be the primary cause of the specific WA if k2 resets correctly, however, the bigger issue is below.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This line resets the count to 1 every time a character is read, overwriting previous counts for the same word. Count should only be initialized when a new word starts, not per character.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This line resets count to 1 every time a character is read, overwriting previous count values. It should only initialize count when starting a new word.]

25.reason: Error: The condition `~(tolower(ch)<='z'&&tolower(ch)>='a')` is incorrect. The `~` operator is bitwise NOT, not logical NOT. It should be `!(tolower(ch)<='z'&&tolower(ch)>='a')` to check if the character is not a letter.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: The condition `~(tolower(ch)<='z'&&tolower(ch)>='a')` is incorrect. The `~` operator is bitwise NOT, not logical NOT. It should be `!(tolower(ch)<='z'&&tolower(ch)>='a')` to check if the character is not a letter.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  bitwise NOT ~ used instead of logical NOT !; though functionally works in this context, it is a logical error

69.reason: Error: This condition assumes all 100 characters match, but words are shorter. It should use strcmp to compare strings, not character-by-character comparison with a fixed length. This may fail to detect duplicate words correctly.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: This condition assumes all 100 characters match, but words may be shorter. It should use strcmp to compare strings, not character-by-character comparison with a fixed length.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: This condition assumes all 100 characters match, but words are shorter. It should use strcmp to compare strings, not character-by-character comparison with a fixed length. This may fail to detect duplicate words correctly.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Condition is incorrect; equality should be checked with strcmp, not with a count of matching bytes
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  condition unlikely to be true due to garbage; use strcmp on null-terminated strings
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Comparing all 100 characters is incorrect because words are shorter and padded with undefined/garbage values or zeros depending on initialization. It should use strcmp or compare up to the null terminator.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Comparing fixed 100 characters instead of using strcmp or checking until null terminator. Since strings are not null-terminated properly and may contain garbage, this comparison is unreliable. Even if they were null-terminated, comparing exactly 100 chars is wrong for words shorter than 100 chars unless padded with zeros, which isn't guaranteed here.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This condition assumes words are exactly 100 characters long. It should use strcmp instead to compare actual string content up to null terminator.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This condition assumes words are exactly 100 characters long and equal only if all 100 chars match. But words are null-terminated and shorter; this leads to false matches or misses. Should use strcmp instead.]
